_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how the starting point of a sentence or paragraph — called the theme — helps guide the reader and create a logical flow in our writing.

_source.w: We are learning how the starting point of a sentence or paragraph — called the theme — helps guide the reader and create a logical flow in our writing.
y: How we begin a sentence or paragraph sets up what the reader expects to hear about next.
Controlling theme positions is a sophisticated writing technique that creates clear, cohesive texts.
This knowledge helps us structure our writing so ideas build on each other logically.
eg: We examine how the first word or phrase in each sentence of a paragraph connects to the overall topic.
We rearrange the opening words of sentences to improve the flow of a paragraph.
We notice how authors use the beginning of paragraphs to signal new ideas or topics.
We rewrite a paragraph to create better cohesion by changing the starting points of sentences.
We identify how transitional words at the start of sentences create connections between ideas.
We practise beginning sentences in different ways to vary our writing style.
